rulings.Case Title: IRYNA KHARCHENKO VS. EXIGEN (USA) INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 20, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT EXIGEN (USA), INC. Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. Each Defendant's motion is granted. The action does not implicate the intellectual property contract; it relates to the employment and mutual arbitration contracts only. While the Court does not find procedural unconsionability, it does find substantive unconsionability in the mutual arbitration contract clause conferring exclusive authority upon the arbitrator to determine enforceability of the arbitration contract and the discovery clauses in (page 2 thereon) severs the "Discovery" paragraph and the work "exclusive" in the "Arbitration Procedures" paragraph. (late tentative) =(302KOPP/PB)

rulings.Case Title: IRYNA KHARCHENKO VS. EXIGEN (USA) INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 20, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT GREG SHENKMAN Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. Each Defendant's motion is granted. The action does not implicate the intellectual property contract; it relates to the employment and mutual arbitration contracts only. While the Court does not find procedural unconsionability, it does find substantive unconsionability in the mutual arbitration contract clause conferring exclusive authority upon the arbitrator to determine enforceability of the arbitration contract and the discovery clauses in (page 2 thereon) severs the "Discovery" paragraph and the work "exclusive" in the "Arbitration Procedures" paragraph. (late tentative) =(302KOPP/PB)

rulings.Case Title: WAI MAN CHAN VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Wriot Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 20, 2009, line 10, PETITIONER WAI CHAN'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Off Calendar. First, Plaintiff served his petition by mail and CCP section 1073 requires service in the same manner as a summons. Second, Plaintiff is seeking administrative mandamus under CCP section 1094.5 but has not lodged the transcript of the underlying hearing. =(302KOPP)
